title: Change RerankerSpecter Dataset question key
body:

# GitHub Actions CI

The Jenkins CI system has been replaced by GitHub Actions self-hosted runners.

The GitHub Actions CI will run automatically when the "Run CICD" label is added to the PR.
To re-run CI remove and add the label again.
To run CI on an untrusted fork, a NeMo user with write access must first click "Approve and run".

# Before your PR is "Ready for review"

**Pre checks**:

- [ ] Make sure you read and followed [Contributor guidelines](https://github.com/NVIDIA/NeMo/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)
- [ ] Did you write any new necessary tests?
- [ ] Did you add or update any necessary documentation?
- [ ] Does the PR affect components that are optional to install? (Ex: Numba, Pynini, Apex etc)
  - [ ] Reviewer: Does the PR have correct import guards for all optional libraries?
  
**PR Type**:

- [ ] New Feature
- [ ] Bugfix
- [ ] Documentation

If you haven't finished some of the above items you can still open "Draft" PR.

## Who can review?

Anyone in the community is free to review the PR once the checks have passed.
[Contributor guidelines](https://github.com/NVIDIA/NeMo/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) contains specific people who can review PRs to various areas.

# Additional Information

- Related to # (issue)

step_plan.summary: Fix Specter reranker data loading by making the Specter datamodule create datasets using `question_key="query"`, matching the JSONL schema emitted by its preprocessing. Update the corresponding unit test to expect the new key and to validate dataset samples can be accessed without key errors.

steps.operation: Align Specter reranker dataset creation with preprocessing output by switching the dataset question key from "question" to "query"
steps.reason: Specter preprocessing writes JSONL records with a `query` field (alongside `pos_doc`/`neg_doc`), but the datamodule currently forces `question_key="question"` when calling `create_reranker_dataset`, causing downstream dataset parsing (e.g., `ReRankerDataset._process_example`) to look up a non-existent key and potentially raise KeyError or produce empty/incorrect samples.
steps.side_effects: Changes the expected schema for Specter-specific datasets created via this datamodule; any custom Specter data that incorrectly used `question` will need to be updated. Improves runtime stability by preventing failures when iterating/indexing the dataset produced by the module’s own preprocessing.

steps.operation: Update Specter reranker datamodule unit test expectations to the new question/query key and add a regression check that dataset indexing works
steps.reason: The test currently asserts that `question_key == "question"` for the Specter datamodule path; after aligning implementation with the emitted JSONL schema, it should assert `"query"`. Adding an index/iteration smoke check (e.g., accessing `train_dataset[0]` or iterating one batch) prevents regressions where the key mismatch reappears and only fails at runtime.
steps.side_effects: Test fixtures or mocks that construct examples may need to include `query` instead of `question`. CI signal improves by catching schema mismatches early; no production behavior change beyond the key alignment already introduced.
